title: Of the Black Belt
venue: A. C. McClurg and Co., Chicago

license.note: Published 1903; public domain in the United States.

abstract: Of the Black Belt (1903), by W. E. B. Du Bois. From The Souls of Black Folk.
excerpt: I am black but comely, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, As the tents of Kedar, as the curtains of Solomon. Look not upon me, because I am black, Because the sun hath looked upon me: My mother’s children were angry with me; They made me...
